5.reason: [Reads newline character as part of string, may cause extra output and incorrect processing]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Reads newline character as part of string, may cause extra output and incorrect processing]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Using scanf("%c") in a loop until EOF does not stop at newline; requires manual EOF (Ctrl+D/Z). Should read the whole line with gets() or fgets().]

10.reason: Loop condition j<=i processes the null terminator at s[i], which may output an extra null character. It should be j<i.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Off-by-one: j should be < i, because s[i] is uninitialized and not part of input; also causes out-of-bounds access]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop runs up to j <= i, but i is the count of characters read, so index i is out of bounds; should be j < i.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition j<=i processes the null terminator at s[i], which may output an extra null character. It should be j<i.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop bound error: should be j < i to avoid accessing the null terminator at s[i]]

15.reason: [Incorrect boundary checks: conditions like `s[j-1]>='0' && s[j+1]<='9'` do not ensure both characters are digits; should also check `s[j-1]<='9' && s[j+1]>='0'` (and similarly for letters). Also, accessing s[j-1] or s[j+1] when j=0 or j=i may cause out-of-bounds access.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Range checks incomplete: only checks one bound for each side, allowing non‑digit/non‑letter characters to be considered valid; also no bounds check for j-1 and j+1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incomplete range checks; both characters must be in the same range. Also missing bounds check for j-1 and j+1.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incomplete range validation. It only checks one bound for each character, failing to ensure both are strictly within the same type range (e.g., missing s[j-1]<='9' and s[j+1]>='0').
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incomplete range validation: missing upper bound check for s[j-1] and lower bound check for s[j+1], which fails to strictly enforce that both characters are within the same specified range]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary checks: conditions like `s[j-1]>='0' && s[j+1]<='9'` do not ensure both characters are digits; should also check `s[j-1]<='9' && s[j+1]>='0'` (and similarly for letters). Also, accessing s[j-1] or s[j+1] when j=0 or j=i may cause out-of-bounds access.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition checks only one side of the range for type validity; e.g., s[j-1] >= '0' but doesn't ensure s[j-1] <= '9', similarly for letters. This allows mixed-type cases like 'a'-'9' to pass incorrectly. Also, when j=0, s[j-1] accesses s[-1], causing undefined behavior.]

18.reason: The <= condition includes the end character s[j+1], which will be printed again in the next iteration, causing duplicate output. It should be < to only print intermediate characters.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition should be < to avoid printing the ending character, which will be printed again later, causing duplication]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Should print only intermediate characters (excluding ending char); condition should be < not <=.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The <= condition includes the end character s[j+1], which will be printed again in the next iteration, causing duplicate output. It should be < to only print intermediate characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Logical error: condition <= causes the end character s[j+1] to be printed twice; should use < since s[j+1] will be printed in the next iteration]
